overview.Summary: The attack begins with a spear phishing email targeting employees with access to SCADA systems. After compromising credentials, attackers execute malware to gain remote access to industrial control systems, leading to disruption of water treatment processes.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Disruption of critical infrastructure as part of a geopolitical conflict

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: An attacker sends a spear phishing email containing a malicious link to employees with access to SCADA systems. The email appears legitimate, tricking the recipient into clicking the link and downloading malware.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 226 Name: Session Credential Falsification through Manipulation
stage.description: Once initial access is established, the malware extracts credentials from memory, allowing the attacker to compromise system accounts and access sensitive information.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 555 Name: Remote Services with Stolen Credentials
stage.description: Using compromised credentials, the attacker accesses internal RDP services, enabling lateral movement within the network to reach critical control systems.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 276 Name: Inter-component Protocol Manipulation
stage.description: Attackers manipulate SCADA protocols to disrupt water treatment processes, potentially leading to environmental hazards or operational downtime.

Scenario.summary: The attack exploits human error and weak security practices to infiltrate critical infrastructure, demonstrating the vulnerability of industrial control systems to advanced cyber threats.
